name: Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular desde Tivenys
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Tivenys offer diverse terrain within the Province of Tarragona, Spain. The region is characterized by the karstic mountain ranges of the Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area, providing rugged landscapes. This area features varied topography, including inland mountain scenery and features akin to a coastal range, making it suitable for different mountain biking skill levels. The Ebro River also influences the local landscape, offering routes that combine river views with mountain challenges. Best mountain bike trails around Tivenys The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular desde Tivenys, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the rugged terrain of the protected natural area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ermita de Sant Joan Baptista loop from Tivenys, a difficult 22.6 miles (36.3 km) path. This loop offers extensive views and challenging climbs through the local mountains. Local mountain bikers also love the Red Bridge of Tortosa – Tortosa loop from Tivenys, a 20.8 miles (33.5 km) trail leading through varied landscapes towards Tortosa, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Tivenys is defined by karstic mountain ranges, diverse topography, and proximity to the Ebro River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Tivenys are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tivenys's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain biking terrain can I expect around Tivenys?
A: The Tivenys area is characterized by the rugged, karstic mountain ranges of the Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area. You'll find diverse topography, from inland mountain scenery to features resembling a coastal range, offering varied trails. The presence of the Ebro River also means some routes combine river views with mountain challenges.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Tivenys region?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Tivenys listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences. These include 5 moderate routes and 6 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate mountain bike trails suitable for less experienced riders in Tivenys?
A: Yes, while many trails are challenging, there are 5 moderate routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31499522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Bridge of Tortosa – 🇪🇦Tortosa loop from Tivenys&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 33.5 km trail that takes about 2 hours and 13 minutes to complete, offering varied landscapes towards Tortosa.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945405355/sierras-de-cardo-el-boix-circular-desde-tivenys" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular desde Tivenys&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 31.6 km route exploring rugged terrain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14892698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Joan Baptista loop from Tivenys&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 36.3 km path with extensive views and challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Tivenys?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945405355/sierras-de-cardo-el-boix-circular-desde-tivenys" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Cardó-el Boix – circular desde Tivenys&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31571857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Bridge of Tortosa – La Font Vella de Alfara loop from Tivenys&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails near Tivenys?
A: The region offers several natural and historical points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda&lt;/a&gt;, a natural warm spring, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3413673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lluminosa Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4995563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Roc Recreation Area and Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; also offers a pleasant stop. The trails often traverse the Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area, known for its unique karstic formations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Tivenys?
A: The mountain bike trails around Tivenys are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Many users praise the varied terrain and the challenging nature of the routes, often highlighting the scenic views and the sense of adventure in the karstic mountains.

Q: Is Tivenys a good base for exploring other attractions in the Province of Tarragona?
A: Yes, Tivenys's location makes it a good starting point for exploring. Beyond the trails, you can visit nearby attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old railway viaduct&lt;/a&gt; or cycle along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6243847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Val de Zafán Greenway&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as Tortosa Cathedral and Miravet Castle are also within a reasonable distance.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area, which many trails traverse, is a Natura 2000 Network Special Protection Area. It's home to various birds of prey and wildlife such as badgers, foxes, weasels, and genets. Keep an eye out, especially in quieter sections of the trails.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer views of the Ebro River?
A: Yes, some routes are influenced by the Ebro River, offering opportunities to combine river views with mountain challenges. While specific river-focused routes aren't detailed, the general landscape description indicates that the river is a prominent feature in the local area, suggesting routes that pass near or offer vistas of it.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tivenys?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate of the Province of Tarragona, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Tivenys, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, and winters are typically mild but can have cooler, wetter periods. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.
tips: A small hermitage in ruins with views of the old Cardó spa.

Old spa located at the top of the Salt del Frare cliff, in the Sierra de Cardó, in the municipality of Benifallet.
Initially, the building was built by some friars and consisted of a small house and a chapel. Later the convent and the 14 hermitages were built, but during the Carlist War the friars had to abandon the building, which was abandoned. It was not until the middle of the 19th century that some businessmen from Tortosa transformed the ruins of the monastery into a spa.
Beautiful panoramic view of the town of Benifallet and the Ebro river from the Cardó-el Boix mountains.
